name: KOMOJU Japan Multi-Payment Gateway API
what_it_does: KOMOJU Japan multi-payment gateway REST API for international merchants entering Japan to accept credit cards, convenience store (konbini) payments, bank transfers, PayPay, PayEasy, and other Japan-specific payment methods through a single unified integration without requiring Japanese corporate entity. Enables AI agents to manage KOMOJU payment session creation for Japan multi-method checkout automation, handle credit card processing for Japan card payment automation, access konbini convenience store payment for Japan cash payment automation, retrieve bank transfer (Furikomi) for Japan bank payment automation, manage PayPay and LINE Pay for Japan mobile wallet automation, handle payment status polling for Japan async payment confirmation automation, access refund and cancellation for Japan order management automation, retrieve webhook notification for Japan payment event automation, manage subscription billing for Japan recurring payment automation, and integrate KOMOJU with international merchants, marketplaces, and games companies for end-to-end Japan payment acceptance automation.
use_cases: Accepting konbini cash payments from Japan e-commerce checkout agents
Processing Japan credit and debit cards from international merchant agents
Offering PayPay and LINE Pay from Japan mobile wallet checkout agents
Polling async konbini payment completion from Japan order management agents
not_for: Japan domestic merchants with Japanese corporate entity (use Stripe JP or GMO for native JPY)
Real-time konbini confirmation (konbini payments complete when customer pays at store — async)
China payment methods (use Alipay or WeChat Pay API for China wallets)
best_when: An international merchant wanting AI agents to automate Japan-specific payment acceptance including konbini, credit cards, PayPay, and bank transfers through KOMOJU's Japan multi-payment gateway without requiring Japanese corporate entity.
avoid_when: KONBINI PAYMENT IS ASYNC — POLLING REQUIRED: Konbini payments complete when customer physically pays at convenience store (up to 3 days); automated order fulfillment must not proceed immediately after konbini payment creation; automated polling or webhook must confirm payment before fulfillment. PAYMENT METHOD SELECTION REQUIRES CUSTOMER CONTEXT: Japan payment method selection (card, konbini, bank transfer) varies by customer preference; automated checkout must present all available methods; automated single-method checkout misses significant Japan customer segments. JAPANESE YEN NO DECIMAL: KOMOJU amounts are in integer JPY (no cents or decimals); automated amount must be integer yen; automated decimal yen amount creates invalid amount error. BANK TRANSFER HAS CUSTOMER-PROVIDED AMOUNT: KOMOJU bank transfer (Furikomi) generates unique bank account for customer; automated bank transfer requires customer to initiate transfer to provided account; automated immediate fulfillment after bank transfer creation creates premature fulfillment before customer completes transfer.

auth.notes: KOMOJU uses Secret Key (HTTP Basic Auth) for API authentication. REST API with JSON. Tokyo, Japan HQ. Founded 2012 by Wes Harmon and Mark Dufour. Acquired by Degica. Products: Credit card, konbini, bank transfer, PayPay, LINE Pay, PayEasy, subscription. SDKs: Ruby, PHP, Python, Node.js, Java, Go. PCI DSS. FSA registered. International merchant friendly (no Japanese entity required). Serves games, manga, and e-commerce.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free test mode; production pricing per transaction
pricing.paid_tiers: Credit card: 3.6%; Konbini: 3.6%; Bank transfer: 3.6%; PayPay: 3.6%; all plus JPY 0 flat fee
pricing.notes: Tokyo JP. Degica subsidiary. Per-transaction fee in JPY. No Japanese entity required. International-friendly.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; payment creation and query APIs throttled; webhooks for payment status events

known_agent_gotchas: KONBINI PAYMENT REQUIRES CUSTOMER EMAIL AND NAME: KOMOJU konbini payment creation requires customer name and email (for konbini slip); automated konbini checkout must collect name and email before creating payment; automated konbini creation without customer info creates missing required field error

ASYNC PAYMENT METHODS REQUIRE WEBHOOK OR POLLING: Konbini, bank transfer, and PayEasy payments complete asynchronously after customer offline action; automated fulfillment must wait for payment.captured webhook; automated immediate post-creation fulfillment creates unfulfilled and unpaid order

PAYMENT SESSION vs PAYMENT DIRECT: KOMOJU supports both hosted payment session (for hosted checkout page) and direct payment API (for custom checkout); automated integration must choose correct approach; automated mixing session URL with direct API creates incompatible flow

EXTERNAL ORDER ID FOR IDEMPOTENCY: KOMOJU external_order_num parameter allows idempotent payment lookup by merchant order ID; automated retry should use external_order_num to find existing payment; automated duplicate payment creation without external_order_num lookup creates duplicate charges

CAPTURE VS AUTHORIZE: KOMOJU supports both immediate capture and authorize-then-capture for cards; automated checkout must configure capture behavior in payment method settings; automated default behavior may differ from expected; explicit capture parameter recommended
agent_readiness.security_notes: Japan payments. PCI-DSS, FSA. Secret Key basic auth. JP. Card and payment method data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Japan payments. PCI-DSS. Secret key. JP. Card and payment method data.
